reviews.review_text:
This game is a real mixed bag for me.
On one hand, the combat is some of the best I've seen and the pawn system is really unique and well thought.
On the other, there are so many baffling decisions that result in an awful player experience. For example, the extremely limited stamina, lack of mounts, limited inventory and the very limited fast travel (especially in the beginning) make the traversing so tedious I've lost all desire to explore. Then there are the quests, where the game mechanics are often so unintuitive that you need to check an online guide how to do something that should have been a press of a button. And even then it seems everybody has a different issues, like NPCs disappearing, not doing what they are supposed to, or simply being killed offscreen by a mob and going to the morgue without you knowing. Even when you've completed a quest they'd usually say something like "Great, now come see me tomorrow", so now you have to pointlessly run to the other part of the city to sleep and then run back on the next day. Yeah, more running.
I'm used to games not holding my hand. In fact I usually prefer it that way, but this feels more like purposely done small annoyances, that in great number really take the joy out of playing. I even dropped it for a while cause I knew that if I hop into it, I'd just spend the next 1-2 hours running between villages, spamming the same attacks at mobs.
Overall I can say I had some fun with it and this could easily be a 10/10 game with some QoL improvements, but the way it is now, I am reluctant to recommend it.

This is probably the most frustrating gaming experience I have personally had. Not because the game is bad mind, Its just that it falls well short of the absolute Masterpiece it should have been. I will give it an upvote if only in the slim hope that I can tip the scales to convince CAPCOM to give this Game a DLC like the first game but from my personal experience, I would have to give this a downvote otherwise. 

The game does SO MUCH right that the places where it falls short are baffling to me. Every Vocation feels like a master piece yet most vocations feel neutered because equip able skills were cut in half. This is probably the best looking Game of the Genre yet the performance is complete balls so most people are stuck playing at settings that make the game look like the first one. The quests feel like they're building up a grand and intricate narrative until most plot points get completely forgotten about in the second half of the game. The World feels very lived in and vibrant until you try to interact with it at which point you realize its all just window-dressing.  You are tasked with infiltration missions more than 3 times in the main story and not a single one of them functions properly. The guards just walk past you, they don't care. In fact no one cares if you throw the entire population of the city to their deaths. It very much feels like a play castle world without consequence. 

The romance system feels completely botched. I feel like I am made to have feelings for this Ulrika girl when I have barely met her like twice and now we are supposed to have a house and hearth together or something. I suppose I cant really complain about that because the romance system has always been hilarious in these games. At least in the first game you could have some really funny moments with it though. this game doesn't really allow for that. The quests are meh. They have always been meh. you dont play these games for the story but its frustrating that this game puts so much emphasis on them as if it isnt aware how mid the characters and their quests are. The Idea of Meisters and Meisters Teachings in every vocation sounds like so much fun in theory until you realize that they basically gutted the good skills from the first game and locked them behind completing these Meister's quests (Some examples of which are genuinely so convoluted and downright ass that I guarantee you, you will lock yourself out of the possibility of getting all of them if you don't refer to a guide) on top of kissing their ass enough to convince them to graciously grant you their skills. Not to mention THE GAME DOESNT TELL YOU WHO THESE MEISTERS ARE SO GOOD LUCK FINDING THEM OR EVEN LEARNING THAT MEISTERS ARE EVEN A THING IN THE FIRST PLACE ON A BLIND PLAYTHROUGH... This entire system has been added to make the vocation experience WORSE from the original game and I just cant wrap my head around why they had to do it this way. They had so much potential with these meisters too. 

The bosses and Creatures are super satisfying to fight, in my opinion the combat of this game rivals and at times exceeds that of monster hunter,  yet between this and the first game, there are only like 3 new creatures to fight and im counting the sphinx among those 3 which 1> isnt even really a fight and 2> 90% of players will completely miss. Not counting the enemies you get to face during a break of the cycle which literally no one has done if you go by steam achievements.

This might be rude to say but as neither  the Dark Arisen DLC for the first game, nor Dragons Dogma Online which are in my opinion the absolute highlights of Dragons Dogma were made by the original Director; Hideaki Itsuno, Mayhaps the best thing that could happen to this game for the players would be a DLC that's similar to Dark arisen, made by another director.

What a tragedy to have all the tools to make a game that could have been remembered for years, and you make this.
What an absolute spit to the face to have made a game that so clearly shows how good it could have been, and its not.
What does it matter if the world is so expanse, so colorfull and rich and graphics are through the roof and innovative, if the main story that drives the game is something that a 13 year old could have written?
All good you can say about this game is underminded by the design choices they took, there are many, you can read them below from ppl that cared more than me, and if you dont take a wrong with that, maybe this game is for you, congratulations!
You would think they would have learned from 1, but they didnt.
Just like MHWilds, someone or something is seriously wrong up at the decision board, these are grave mistakes, these are not made at the last week of the game... you make the game with these ideas in mind... is this the best you could came up with? 
Really?
Anyways, this was the last one Capcom, no more faith in you or your other titles, you wronged me twice, you will have to work hard to earn my trust back and see any money from me. If you even care that is. 
Hopefully ppl will wisen up, but I doubt it.

The original Dragons Dogma is a game that i have bought so many times it's slightly embarrassing. 
I love that game so much, despite having a very basic narrative that is meant to be light on purpose for you to forge your own adventure. It's a 9/10 game in my eyes and left me wanting more.

When Dragons Dogma 2 was announced i was so bloody excited, finally i could get that sense of discovery and adventure that i desired all over again, but hopefully with better graphics, performance and gameplay mechanics. 

I initially bought and finished the game on PS5, and i immediately fell in love with it. It provided me with more Dragons Dogma with pretty much everything i loved from the first game with extra layers of gameplay on top. 

The game is beautiful and exploration was both amazing and horrifying (at night).
The music when used is affective and nothing to complain about. 
Gameplay was more dragons dogma and for me, was amazing,

The unfortunate thing that really lowers the bloody overall score for me, was the performance....
I have never been so in love, yet pissed the hell off at a game like this before..... 
Even on PS5 before being patched i had crashes and extreme slow downs. 
Despite that, i pushed through and was rewarded. 

After finishing it on PS5, i wanted more.... hoping the PC version would give me more flexibility to tweak settings and avoid being ass blasted by crashes and severe performance drops...

I WAS WRONG.

PC version is playable, but holy ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ it's not optimized worth a damn. 

Is it an awesome game? Yes, it's a 9/10 game for me....
Is it a game i'd recommend on PC unless you got a really good gaming rig? No... ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ no.

Please bear in mind that these are my opinions only, but i feel like a guy who went out on a promising date with a beautiful woman..... only to get puked on at the end of the night, and had to drag her drunk ass into a Taxi.

Dragon's Dogma 2 is a phenomenal game - Mixed reviews for this are insane. 

Game starts off strong, with an extremely detailed character creator - create any character you want. 

Dragon's Dogma oozes atmosphere. The world is beautiful and discovering new locations is fun. Wondering monsters and the day/night/weather cycle make the world feel alive. Having an ogre find your level 1 party in the middle of the night is terrifying. Everything comes together to form memorable adventures. You'll constantly encounter other players creations along the way, with their unique appearance, making it feel like a living world.

Beginning of the game is less grindy and offers more guidance than the original Dragon's Dogma.
The vocation system is flexible, and offers a variety of playstyles -archers, wizards, fighters, thiefs etc. Thief was probably my favourite, but each will change how you experience the world.

Ran the game in 1440p with high settings, DLSS on, and Ray-tracing off. *Mostly* performed at 60fps, but dropped in some areas, especially the main city. Keep in mind the minimum requirements for this game are 8gb of vram - that's to run it in 1080p. Didn't ruin the game for me. If you want perfect performance on high settings you're going to need at least 12gb of vram.

Don't buy the DLC. It's disgusting. It's like Nintendo asking you to spend real money on mushroom caps for the next Mario game. All the DLC are common items and easy to get. Total rip off.

The game isn't perfect, and there's definitely a few things to criticize. However, most QOL adjustments you'd want to see in the game have been modded in. Overall, loved the experience. Don't play it for the story, play it for the gameplay and exploration.

It’s fine, but honestly it just feels like playing Dragon’s Dogma again with upgraded graphics and a few modern quality of life improvements.

The story is basically a mirror of the first game. There’s barely anything new here. On top of that, it’s way too short. I genuinely thought I was only halfway through when suddenly I hit the ending and the game was over. The alternate endings add maybe another hour at best, but that’s about it.

Gameplay feels smoother and less clunky, and the new classes are pretty fun to use, but the older vocations barely got any new skills. There are also no real new monsters it’s mostly the same enemies from the first game with updated models.

Sadly, this game isn’t what the trailers sold us. It’s definitely not worth full price, and honestly not even worth it at 50% off.

A massive shame that DD2 features no major gameplay innovations from Dark Arisen. That being said, this isn't some generic dime a dozen annual franchise. There really isn't any other game that does everything Dragon's Dogma does, so I'll gladly accept more of it, even if it's more of the same. Not sure how you could love the first game and not enjoy this one.

Really insane that it got a second chance, but I sadly don't think it will ever get another.

The game and world is super fun and engaging when I can get it to run. Unfortunately, even during the first month or so after launch, I was plagued with performance issues and crashes. I held out hope that Capcom would eventually fix these but even two years down the road, the game hard crashes my monitors even after doing multiple display setting / graphical tweaks and full tech troubleshooting of my drivers. I still enjoy the actual gameplay when I can get it to run but the technical issues go from an "inconvenience" to "do not recommend" for me as Capcom doesn't seem to be making headway in fixing these issues.

To start it off, I really like this game. I'm still midway through, but the general consensus I have here is that Dragon's Dogma 2 doesn't really try to "appeal to everyone". Devs definitely had a vision, resulting in a final product that is perhaps a bit old-school, and not very convenient at times. You can see this in the single autosave for the campaign; the absence of fast travel; the absence of crucial UI, like the current time and favourability meters for NPCs; the simplified stealth and prison mechanics; and much more. Many of these issues, coupled with SUBPAR optimisation, would DESTROY the enjoyment of many players, so having sunk 66 hours into the game, I can totally understand why the reviews are "Mixed". 

As for myself, I enjoy the game immensely. I suppose I just happen to fall into its "target audience". One autosave? Perfect! For the first time in many years, I won't force myself to save-scam for the best rewards, outcomes, etc. I'm FREE! It's really great and doesn't waste my time anymore.
Same with UI. Sometimes less is more, and having less UI, for instance, makes me want to keep track of the sun & stars to plan my rest and combat. It's so cool, lol. Exploration and combat are generally DD2's strongest sides. Even after 66 hours, it doesn't feel old, which is more than enough for a game like this IMO.

Quests were good enough for me. There were interesting ones and "fetch" quests, too. But ultimately, the "sense of journey" was retained, and that's what matters most, isn't it?
Regarding Pawns, most people are disappointed with them. Many say they're "not that smart" and that it's false advertising. Personally, I don't care about broken promises - pawns are still useful, the concept is still amazing and fresh, and I still have a lot of fun exploring the world alongside them. Yes, your companions aren't human-level smart or AI-driven. They run on an algorithm - who would have guessed that, huh?

Regarding the optimisation. I have a good rig, I DON'T use DLSS or FG, and I haven't had a single crash throughout my entire playthrough. DD2 looks gorgeous - a feast for the eyes. I can't be sure about running it with upscalers, though, because the DLSS here is quite bad - it blurs and ruins the image. I tried it a bit and had a lot of ghosting, and other trails and image smearing. I haven't tried optimising the settings, so it might still be good even with this AI tech.

One more thing I would like to add. Precisely because of the game's "vision" and the desire to steer clear of the general audience, I experienced many moments of complete adoration for its design, and felt immense gratitude towards the genius behind it. An unexpected new class or ultimate skill to play around with was just "randomly unlocked" after a cool sequence; an unexpected new quest with the most bizarre and fascinating creature to play you around; an unexpected new item that lets you do crazy things you thought were impossible, whether it's a weapon or just a regular item; an unexpected new location in the middle of nowhere - I'm constantly showered with these, and it's exhilarating!

All in all, I'm really happy with my purchase, especially since it was on sale with like a 50% discount - definitely worth the buck now imo. And again, HUGE respect to the studio which, in 2026, decided not to create a "product for everybody", but to "craft a game that resonates with a few". This is so freaking awesome, it gives me hope for humanity <3

Only recommended on a deep sale. Feels like a dumbed down graphical update of the first game. The combat is fun, but at least before I quit playing, it felt like there were about 3 enemy types total to fight, so it quickly got stale. Not worth $70 to me.

I enjoyed it. Works well on my linux machine, and most negative reviews are about micro transactions or performance issues, i suffered no performance issues but my machine is relatively high end. I never interacted with any micro transactions throughout the game and i do not feel like i missed out or had a negative impact because of it.
Boring. Remake of the first game with a less interesting cast and bosses- especially early on.

NG+ and still hooked and here's why:

Dragon's Dogma 2 is the most organic open world I've ever played. Most open-world games rely on scripted spectacle to impress you, DD2 just lets the world live. The Pawn system, the AI, the emergent encounters, it all feels genuinely dynamic in a way I haven't seen before. The graphics are also a massive glow-up from the original.

The strongest aspect of this game is exploration, and the simulation-heavy design just makes it endlessly fun, the world is always throwing something unexpected at you.

The one fair downside? Content. It doesn't match Dogma 1 + Dark Arisen in sheer volume, and that's worth being honest about. Honestly, most of the negative reviews come from Dogma 1 fans holding it to that standard, which is fair. There's a DLC rumored to be in the works which could change things, but we'll see.

Also, if you didn't enjoy Dogma 1, DD2 is not going to win you over. This game is very much for a specific kind of player, and it knows it.

That said, the foundation here is just king. The combat is weighty and fun, the world feels tangible, reactive and dynamic, and the core loop stays satisfying well into NG+.

I understand the hate behind this title. It was hyped before its release, people were expecting this to be the game of the year. Majority of the people didn't even know what Dragon's Dogma was about or what kind of a title it was. Everyone thought another Skyrim or a standart RPG with an open world was on the horizon. Thus, the disappointment. Dragon's Dogma 2 is what it is. If you have played and enjoyed DD1, you may enjoy this. If you're a newcomer to this title and if you're expecting from an RPG:
Good writing
A lot of dialogue with meaningful impact
A good story
Handholding
Quest marks openly telling you what to do
Easy fast travel

THIS GAME IS NOT FOR YOU!

Dragon's Dogma is a very unique title and it's very niché. This game has:
Amazing combat
Exploration
Unique systems such as Pawns
Punishing/Rewarding fights

Also, there are many comments about microtransactions. They mean nothing in this game, every item that you can buy can also be collected easily in-game. The game doesn't force you to buy these items from the store. I have played more than 30 hours so far and I didn't feel to buy anything by paying real money. They are just there because Capcom does this with every title! Even in RE4 Remake, they're trying to sell items. Do you need them? Of course not.

I'm not trying to defend Capcom or DD2. I'm just trying to clarify some points and I'm trying to help the people who wants to give a chance to DD2. 

IF YOU ARE COMING FROM DD1 HERE ARE THE NEGATIVE PARTS
DD2 has its flaws and some dumbed down systems I wish they never changed. The vocation system is much simplified. Mixed classes are gone. Such as drifter. You can use only one type of weapon with each class in the game. Also, we had 8 active skills in DD1, now we have to rely on 4 skills. 
Also, the itemization system is changed. We could wear 9-10 parts of clothes in DD1, in this one you can only wear a helmet, a body armor, a leg armor and a cape. That's it. 
Enemy variety is pretty poor. Saurians, Goblins, Wolves and Harpies are everywhere just like DD1. I expected a lot more from a sequel.
No quest boards with this one. You can still do escort missions etc. however you encounter the npcs on the map this time. (Which is worse in my opinion)

POSITIVE PARTS
The visuals are amazing, the atmosphere, the music, it's all there. 
The Pawns are much smarter and much more impactful.
The combat feels much smoother and better than DD1.
Exploration is much more enjoyable with this one, the map is fun to traverse. 
Ox Carts are a good addition, they are basically a cheap way of fast travel.
The fights feel epic. This is still the strongest point of the game.
Character customization is super detailed. 

PERFORMANCE ISSUES
DD2 is poorly optimized to this day after 2 years. The game crashes if you don't start it by running it in administrator mod. 
I'm running a 5060 TI 16 GB, without DLSS and all maxed settings. My fps is 55-60 in the cities and mostly higher than 60 while exploring. 
DLSS, even in quality mod is useless. So, if you have somewhat a weak pc. Stay away! Using DLSS is not a good solution in this title, the game looks blurry and man trust me when I say this, the game looks even worse than DD1 when you use DLSS.

To sum up, if you're a DD fan. You should definitely play this. You probably squeezed the hell out of DD1 by now, so this is worth giving a try. This is still the game we love. All the reasons to love this game are still there. 

If you aren't a DD fan, again, give it a try. It is a unique game that you can't find anything like this elsewhere.

If you are here for a story, good written dialogues and meaningful quests. I'm sorry, you'll not like this game. You better stay away.

cant recommend anything that decides to use denuvo. it only ♥♥♥♥♥ performance and gives issues to those that actually purchase their ♥♥♥♥. genuinely had better performance on my pirated version. these anti piracy measures dont work and only hurt you the consumers.

The game is in a terrible state with no updates, communication or directions from the developers or capcom. The game is buggy and poorly optimised and definitely not worth your money, even on sale. The combat is fun but thats about the only saving grace from this game. Until any update is done to the game and more content is added, this game will remain as a none recommendation.

I feel like the kind of people that don't like this game are the same kind of people that struggle with simple addition.

I got into this game around the same time I started playing DND and the way I describe this game is like a DM that initially comes off as harsh but allows you as much freedom as you want and rewards you for breaking the game.
If you try to rush through this game as fast as you can and try to force it to play by your rules, you will NOT have a good time. But if you take the time to learn the game and play by the rules it sets and you aren't afraid to ask questions and most importantly PAY ATTENTION, you will realise that this game is one of the better action RPGs out there.
The combat heavily encourages experimentation, building a proper team is essential, expecially in the early game and you need to come in with the mind set that you are going on an adventure, not that you are playing a video game.

Summery
Take the time to learn the game and you'll have a great time, but if you come expecting to have a power fantasy with no effort put in on your part, you'll end up writing a very winey review.

I completed my first playthough (With some side quests) in 40 hours.

Was not one that had experienced the first game; unsure if that contrast was why people have left so many brutal reviews. Saw this game being played from a streamer on tiktok and immediately recognized the style as something I would be into as I was in-between games at the time. Takes a bit to adjust to the mechanics, but it's phenomenal, unique party system, beautiful graphics, intriguing story, and open world interface; not to mention mod friendly, 10/10 for me.

I loved the first one. Absolutely some of the most amazing classic action rpg feeling world and gameplay, you really felt like an adventurer. It had some extremely weird quirks and baffling development choices but a lot of that added to the charm. 

Fast forward and DD2 comes out. ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ Imagine what they can do with the game with the talent and tech we have now so far into the future!

Turns out, it runs like crap, it's almost the exact same quirky baffling design decisions with really strange pacing and structure of quests with YET AGAIN the most overly pointless paid dlc as is common with capcom. HOWEVER. It DOES have the most satisfying combat and exploration in an open world RPG ever. Its so immensely satisfying fighting things and learning new skills feels so exciting to add new moves to fight enemies with. 

Too bad they abandoned it with no DLC planned it seems (which is a shame cause the DLC from the first game was some of the BEST post game content for an rpg ive ever experienced) and to this day still isnt optimized well, and becomes super fun if you're a hardcore fan but really really REALLY hard to recommend to new players or anyone interested. Such a shame.

I hate that unmoored world overwrites your save and I can't go back to finish the quests. I'm disappointed by this decision enough to make a negative review.

Other than that, the game is great.

I just bought this game TODAY and oh my god... After hearing all the complaints and reading posts from back in 2024, I genuinely thought they would have fixed these optimization and crash issues by now. But NOPE!!! I’m running an RTX 5070, yet I can't even reach the character creation screen. The moment I boot it up, the game crashes with a VRAM out-of-memory error. After tweaking and troubleshooting for nearly 3 hours, it either crashes instantly upon launch or just hits me with a black screen on the second attempt.

Cash grab.

I want to play, but two years on and it still crashes constantly. I don't recall the last time I played a game that had problems like this.

i was curious how the DDO adventure went since i never got to play and this game seem to show just that and did more than what i thought it was going to do, give more dogma. NAW. The end game spot is the key and answer to the first game, and lets u fkn play it . this is a worthy sequel and i was about to give up on most modern games but this  right here.....is Dragon dogma 2. All these modern games being coming out with problems and seeing this at launch, and then what they did to monster hunter, i was gonna pass from buying anything capcoom on pc but these reviews changed that mindset. Lossless scaling is clutch for venworth but other than that spot and with the settings to med, high, really shocked how smooth this game runs but this game comes with insane input delay. thank god for nividia reflex. crazy i'm at this many hours on play thru 2 yet the first game.... easily over 10 being at 160h. think i might be stuck on this one for a while. especially since on the ps3, i fked up my stridder so screw warrior, pc made the strongest assassin and stridder but just wasn't feeling war for bitter black isle but this game's warrior is so destructive, it' keeping me off my main just to train my pawn.

This is why I don't like a binary thumbs up or down rating system, because I loved this game. But in its current state, I can't recommend it.
Ran om a 3080ti, by all means not the fastest card in the world anymore, but I'd expect it to run a 2 year old game without getting crazy stuttering after an hour of gameplay, even on Low settings(settings did not impact this at all).

That said, the game itself is at best amazing. I loved the first game, and alot of what I loved remains here. I was totally hooked for the first 30 or so hours, before I slowly started to grow tired of the experience.
The combat is really fun, but there's alot of it. The classes are all distinct and fun to play and experiment with, and the Pawn system is unique and fun.
My issues are with all the glitches and bugs. There are weird things going on all over. In one situation I couldn't win a quest because a random important plot NPC had gotten themselves killed off screen, and there's no indication in game to tell me what had happened.
This is the best kind of sandbox and the worst kind of sandbox at the same time.

Honestly, I had more fun with the first game. Dragon's Dogma and Dark Arisen may not be for everyone still, but I'd recommend that over its sequel. Sadly, for when DD2 is at its peak, its some of the most fun I've had in a game lately.

2 years ago on a ♥♥♥♥♥ computer I played through this game in lag and stutter...

I love this game.

However now when I have an amazing computer I got to level23 and now everytime I try to start up the game it freezes my computer completely to the point where I have to just force shutdown the pc... ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ its frustrating...

Can't find any solutions to this problem either...

I played this game on release and had a terrible time with it.

I loved the first Dragons Dogma (played it A LOT on my old Xbox 360, where I actively killed the UR-Dragon almost everyday for months lol). However, I feel that, in DD2, Capcom removed everything good with DD1, while potencializing everything bad.

The enemies are repetitive, combat is a slog (at least when playing mage or sorcerer - everything takes forever to kill, while spells take forever to cast and are not satisfactory to throw at enemies, at least in my opinion), and it takes forever to get to point A to point B on foot (yes, you can unlock "carriages" to fast travel, but I feel like the exploration on foot should be a lot better).

If you try to explore random caves, dungeons etc., you will likely get frustrated, because most of them are a repetition of the same enemies and crappy loot.

The game's optimization is terrible from day 1, and it does not look like it will improve.

I did not finish the game and don't plan to do it anytime soon. Wouldn't recommend it even on a sale.

This was such a great RPG experience. Remind me of how i fall in love with the first game. The gameplay is superb and hardcore(annoying) in some parts. The standard ending story is too simple then the second part came, it wrap up everything and became really good. Too bad the game literally crash and have terrible performance alongside with demanding high-end hardware, making most people inaccessible or give up before it gets good. Only play it if you have the patience and good PC.

Horrible optimization for such known company and that not even mentioning while playing this it destroy my gpu drivers somehow i had to do clean install and it just freeze when loading last quick save so i have to load Inn save to play it

After sinking over 200 hours into Dragon’s Dogma 2, I still haven’t finished the game… and I genuinely have zero plans to. Look, the story should probably stay in the background until they release DLC. What this game actually brings to the table is hands-down the best "RPG Combat Simulator" out there. Keep that in mind — it’s the best game.

Mind you, I’m playing on a toaster. The optimization is pretty bad, but that’s exactly why we have the modding community.

combat was great fun, world was well built and thought out, exploration made more fun by side quests but would never recommend. crashed 50+ with a D3D fail locking up my whole computer forcing manual restarts of my whole system. Horribly optimized

The mechanics I didn't like in the first Dragon's Dogma are still here, I didn't like some of the features of character creator and armors/equipment, also didn't liked they took off the daggers from the archer vocation... It would have been nice if they had taken into account all the comments the cummunity made for the first game, but... I'm still enjoying it quite a lot.

DD2 is better than par, but not on par with DD1/DDDA. The level, POI, and environmental design seems generic or bland, like they hired a mobile MMO level designer, and not as hand-crafted in the same fashion as you would expect of a SP game of such caliber or the first game. The environments, POIs, etc. of the first game felt intentional and inspired awe, there was grandeur and majesty. This made exploration rewarding as you were engrossed in the world. The introductions of large monsters also had build-up, you met the cyclops if you found it behind the waterfall, the griffin on a quest, the ogre in the cave, in DD2 it didn't feel as special, spawn placement felt random. The world of DD2 is bigger, but interesting POIs were replaced with more dungeons and caves. The variety of enemies and unique POIs might match the first in terms of metrics, but since DD2 is bigger, it should have scaled with it. The first game also had better quests, a war with goblins, Julien/Mercedes, cultists, etc. DD2 quests felt like generic MMO quests, lacking depth. Lastly, DD2 isn't bad, it is in fact better than good, just could've been better -- as a sequel should escalate in scope and craftsmanship vs its predecessor -- in this case, they likely did not have the time and budget.

A Great goodbye to CAPCOM by Hideaki Itsuno. Without ever experiencing the original Dragon's Dogma, I truly feel, that DD2 is Amazing Action-RPG. Although it leans more into the Action part of the genre, then the actual RPG, it doesn't really matter. The Combat system is the King in this game and honestly, I'd love to have more RPGs (or games overall) which provides similiar or more in depth approach to combat, like Dragon's Dogma 2 does. With Exploration, unique Pawn System, Vocations, freedom for players with no limits and more tough approach by the game with almost zero handholding, it truly is Fantastic and unique experience.
Yeah, the technical issues are still prevailing even after 2 years, but I think it's quite good in its current state. I mean, it is almost a miracle  that with so many systems and huge open-world with many Dungeons, cities and monsters is the game even functional xd
Thank you Itsuno-san and the whole team.

Huge fan of the series. Take my money! 

Have yet to finish the game because of side content/exploration, leveling vocations, etc. It can be a huge game if you allow it to be. Crossing my fingers for an expansion like Dark Arisen from the first game.

Without mods, it has been fun to me. Way more fun with mods if the base gameplay leaves you wanting more QoL and fun stuff (like custom spells, vocations and abilities among plenty other stuff to mess around with).

Shockingly Dragon's Dogma 2 is a better game than the original! It's far more polished it just about every aspect. The pacing of world exploration is greatly improved, the game is even quicker to get into the action, each vocation has a more unique identity, the NPC AI is some of the best I've seen in any game, the interactions that you and enemies have with the environment and each other are always impressive, and the story manages to deepen the world set up by of both of the previous titles (Dark Arisen and Online). And like other titles, DD2 continues to take a seemingly generic fantasy action rpg premise and turn it into one of the most mechanically engaging experiences on the market. More than anything DD2 absolutely NAILS gamefeel.

A few things still bothered me: I had to install a mod to prevent my controller from causing constant stutters. Nearly every Capcom game on steam has this issue fir me. I'm also still not a fan of limited inventory space, or limited stamina outside combat. There are many more great mods that fix nearly all the issues I have though.

The most frictional element of DD2 is probably how many actions have permanent consequences, restricting access to progress on certain quests without your knowledge. That aspect and the variety of vocations create a lot of replayability, but it can be frustrating if you're not ready for it. Still much better quest design than something like Dark Souls though!

I hope they make a hard mode and/or dlc and/or third (fourth) game.

it has not so bad visual and some good gameplay features, but also some unpleasant as well as purely disgusting features. capcom should have invested money they put into breasts physics into proper storytelling, main character lines (at least written, if not voiced) and technical details. playing it could be an okayish way to pass a boring evening (if you already bought it) if this "game" worked properly without crushing
definitely not worth the money and this game definitely put me off buying any other capcom games

Very frustrating. bad save system that results in getting hard locked out of quests or decisions until your next playthrough. flat combat, bad exploration, very few enemy types. bad inventory system. characters suck. just generally bad.

First of all, let me say that DD2 does NOT deserve a mixed review. The game is as fun and addicting as the first one. Is it better than the first? In some aspects yes, in others no, but still a very good game on its own merit.

In my 130h I haven't experienced a single crash on 1080p, Ray Tracing OFF, DLSS OFF, ( Motion Blur, Depth of Field, Bloom visual diarrhea OFF) everything else MAX. Stable 60 fps 85% of the game, even in big cities. The rest 15% is, yeah, DD2 is a bit unoptimized but nothing crazy bad. With the amount of NPCs friendly/hostile (their pathfinding) The game manages to work well MOST of the time is credit where credit is due.

With a new Engine for the sequel the Devs chose to go for Realism over Style. Pro: It looks good. Con: It feels blend in comparison to DD1.
The game feels a bit unpolished. Floaty character movement, constantly sliding off terain when you try to grab ledges, etc.

Some design choises feel like the Devs overcompensating for DD1 criticism.
- NPCs constantly initiating a conversation with the player, so you won't miss out on quests. Feels realistic but gets annoying really fast.
- Said system also indirectly impacts Pawns offering themselves for hire on the road, even if your party is full. You can prevent it by having your weapon drawn, but it can get annoying as hell regardless.
- To many enemies every step of the way. In DD1 enemy placement felt consistant even if the total enemy count was on the low side. In DD2 you can get jumped buy goblins, wolves, saurians and whatnot all placed, what feels like, two steps away from each other, which is really hard to deal with in the beginning.

The difficulty in DD2 at endgame is the same as DD1, you're strong enough to just demolish everything. At the early-mid game though, DD2 definitly feel harder despite not having a dedicated ''Hard'' diff. Why? Because of exactly one mechanic, and that is the stagger. DD1 also had stagger, but in 2 it was cranked up to 11. Every time you get staggered, you want to personally travel to Capcom HQ and tell the employees what you think about their moms.

The world in DD2 is about 3 times larger than DD1. The overall sense of exploration is well preserved. Pro: it's seamless (no loadings). Con: The ''Dungeon'' identity is lost. The vast majority is just a bunch of, what feels like, copy pasted caves.

DD2 adds a new fast travel, the Oxcart. Pay up, doze off, wake up to a Griffin landing on you full force, survive, walk the rest of the way on foot. Jokes aside, it's a good addition to the game. In exchange, access to portcrystals (teleport stones) was reduced.

Vocations are mostly the same with some minor changes to certain skills. Rogue/Archer getting separated is a Pro.
The balancing is, again, non-existent. Every class has a broken skill (or 2) that you're just going to spam. This is why I don't see an issue in going from 6 skil slots (DD1) to 4.
The new Trickster class is absolutely busted, but boring. If you want to chill and let your Pawns do all the work.

Overall Dragons Dogma 2 is a Solid 7.5/10.

Not a bad game at all but definitely a niche one for a specific audience.
Very slow dopamine dripper but when it drips it drips good.

My experience is: Encumbered from low inventory space walking simulator without fun or intuitive inventory management. Majority of play time will be spent trying to minimize your storage space, slowly walking across the map and finding chests that give you items that encumber you - and then a small percentage will be fighting super cool enemies with good monster hunter-lite combat and good ragdoll physics. Performance is passing.

This game is a solid 7/10.  The AI is super wonky, if you ask a pawn to show you a chest it will run off a cliff, not every time but a decent amount of time. Besides that I had a lot of fun with it and hoping for a DLC drop soon.

I loved the open world, the character creation, the pawns. There are a lot of places and caves to explore. The graphics are amazing.  It would be even more amazing if it didn't start crashing at around level 30.  Consistent crash on what I would call the loading screen and sometimes in game.  It can be worked through and slowed down but I never got it stopped completely. I ended up sleeping before quitting so I did not lose progress.  Still I have played it through twice and started a third time. I lowered the textures and that helped a lot. I updated drivers, turned off overlays, uninstalled Nvidia app went through the whole list of suggestions and none of that worked what worked for me was lowering textures.

I really don't understand the bad reviews this game has been getting lately.

Is it because the developer isn't releasing a DLC that players are asking for?
Is it really because the game isn't well optimized?
Is it due to a lack of skills that players don't have yet at the beginning of the game?

Is it a shame that no DLC has come out yet? 
I think that with the various endings of the game, the story has been written. And yes, of course, more content can always be added in the form of other monsters, more vocations, especially the one that was used so frequently in DD1.

I use a GPU with 16 GB without any frills whatsoever. I play this game with "raw" game performance and with all other settings on high.
The game runs flawlessly, apart from a single crash so far—2 in all the gaming hours I have spent.

And yes, monsters can often be cast out, and in large numbers at the same time. But with the right skills and with supported pawns who also have the right skills, they can be defeated.

In short, I love this game and every game has something that isn't optimal, but is that a reason to give  a negative review based on a few disappointing aspects? In my opinion that is so unnecessary; special when you played this game for hours and hours. 
That is why I ask the question: where does the real anger come from among those who gave a thumbs down?

Horrible optimisation. Game crashes constantly on the Character creator, which by the way WAS THE MAIN FEATURE OF THE OPEN BETA. I have crashed 3 times trying to create the main character, crashed another 5 times trying to create the pawn. See the 9 hours yeah? Still not past the tutorial. 

I do NOT recommend this game cause it just doesn't work.

I did enjoy first few hours in. But there are glitches where the two handed sword goes missing- I must have given it to one of the pawns that i hired to use and the pawn got swapped later with other pawn. So I lost the two handed sword forever not allowing me to become two handed sword class unless i find another two handed sword. The graphics are nice but you can see noise in the far away- like old television buzzing dots happening in the background I found very distracting. Exploration was fun at first but it does get repetitive. The monsters you meet are pretty similar and I got bored of the combat. even if I got it 50% on sale, i think i could have bought it for 70% off, that would have been worth the purchase for what its worth

game is phenominal in my opinion, i love having a squad of people with me, in the next dragons dogma game id really like to see the main pawns be able to learn and use every vocation that you can, also CAPCOM hint hint can you take the pawn system and copy it into a superhero themed game , fully cusomizable super powers and main pawn superhero , just saying if done right would earn a bit of coin for your troubles.

The graphics are better than the first game (which I felt already had good graphics), but the character creation is worse. There's less creative freedom in the style of character you can make when compared against the first game, and the hair colour options, for example, are utterly abysmal. I'm disappointed, before even starting the actual game.

This game could have been one of the best "Fellowship RPG" game 😭, but the amount of hoop i have to go though just to make it even playable on pc especially the camera issues. and still it being a walking simulator 90% of the time very tedious especially when you don't have a lot of time to spare. i have such terrible UX with it. I know it made to be Console Controller first but it legit not good for pc players. very disappointed😢

Honestly one of the worst story telling I have ever experienced in a game, its truly so ♥♥♥♥. you do so much stuff that just doesnt matter. to then get a vague weird ending that has no impact on the entire journey you just made.
the game is literally designed for a one day playthrough filled with four hours of unique content. its totally monotony game
First game was better in pretty much every way. Big letdown here.

Classic-mid story, really bad open world, mid combat, overall good fantasy world and idea but its just empty. Game feels like a 2015 game idk, everything felt bad ngl. Not for me I guess...

I love this game with all my heart, I really do. However, it is deeply frustrating that after more than two years, we have only received a few minor updates. There is no official DLC and recently, I just caught wind of a co-op mode that is being developed by fans as a hobby project rather than the actual developers. 

I can't get into Monster Hunter or Resident Evil, so it hurts to see Capcom seemingly abandon the one franchise of theirs that I truly care about. It is a masterpiece in the making, but the lack of long-term support is dissapointing. Please stop abandoning games that make your company great.
150 hours including offline play and I can only say 2 words, such disappointment
Great combat, zero quality of life elements.
Peak, 10/10. The game knows what it wants to be and nails it in every way.

It is fun having new different mechanic from DDDA, but some are missing instead (not including BBI). The minus I notice is like, Skills slot, are too few, making it hard to choose. The arrow became really small choice. Some monster even vanished absurdly while fighting if we take too much time. 

I feel like having more skill slot instead a really good skill (meister) that almost kill everything (even dragon) in one shot is better.

The combat is so fun and addicting just like the first game which is one of my favorite games of all time. The questing however doesn't take advantage of the fun gameplay and instead opts to have you do a bunch of sneaking around in a castle for some reason. DLC probably never happening :(((((

Dragon's Dogma is such an amazing title- lore, combat, even philosophically. Sad that Capcom neglects it, and very frustrating because the game has a ♥♥♥♥ ton of unrealized potential.

Wish the game has more to offer but I'm enjoying it. I like the clunk (the UI which is just as clunky as DD1), it matches the gritty, not-so-neat atmosphere that a dark-ish fantasy should have. I dislike some of the smaller changes like Rifts don't heal you anymore, and you can't sit down with the pawn at the inn. Combat feels about the same. I wish we could at least sit on all the chairs in this second game.

I had a bit of trouble getting the game to not crash due to graphic intensity. But it runs fine now. Downloaded a DD2 Save Manager to back up saves.
so far it has been an experience starting out, alot to comprehend and master

Almost 20 hours in and I'm quite enjoying the gameplay and environments. The story seems solid so far - but its still early! A little sad there are only two romance options, both female - but I'll live lol
Don't know why people have been b**ching. The game scratches the same itch DD1 did, and more. Good stuff.
I'm dogmaing it, I'm dogmaing it, I'm dogmaing it, oh no! the plastic grigori escaped the jar! NOOOOOOO!!!
Even after all these years it still runs like ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. Just can't bring myself to finish the game.

"2026 past updates this is still a great game with great performance problems."
Dragon's Dogma 2 has a nice enjoyable cliche story with good environment and a solid, fluid and fun combat beside his gameplay loop, on top of all that still has one of the worst optimizations ever made, even if you have a top tier PC still is going to be poor.
solid action combat and better adventuring than the first game. Dont fall for the microtransactions.

I am only starting to play, but I am actually impressed by the play of this game. I thought it might be another "Hit B for action to complete" type game that attempted to have extreme console graphics thus limiting the actual ability to play a game.  This was great with Dragon's Lair back in the 80's, but really it is not very much fun to play that way now - and this game was NOT at all like that.  The combat is far more fun and in all it is a great start to a game. I'll amend when I actually get some decent hours with it, but for now, I would recommend it to anyone that enjoys RPGs with some action.  Playing to fill my RPG itch between BG3 hours and I really do like it.  I think it could fill what I need for some time! Amazing looking game, also. The developers created something very cool.

This game is not perfect. But to me that's what makes a game worth playing. It has charm and immense amount of character and enjoyment. This game gives me the same feelings that Skyrim did when I first played it. This will go down as one of my most enjoyed experiences in gaming. I hope that one day they decide to add DLC or make a third installment to the series. 10/10

This game has been out for a good while now and despite having a decent setup the game starts stuttering consistently after playing for a bit and can only be fixed with a pc reboot. on top of that certain items are pointless because you still can't modify the item quickslots. additionally this game's combat feels like it was designed around a lock-on system but doesn't have one.

This game is great for the first 20-30 hours. The combat is fun at first but once you're strong enough it gets repetitive and boring. The bosses are repetitive and the mobs are definitely boring. The story and the characters are vomit-inducingly bad - the writing and performances are horrendous. This game definitely doesn't warrant the price tag. That said, I enjoy playing it every once in a while and I think most people would enjoy it but once the cracks start to show, you need to take a super long break. I wish there was a neutral button, because I both recommend and not recommend this title.
game runs well, easy to get distracted and waiste several days exploring

I hardly can’t tell if this was a remaster or new game. It is similar to the 1st game in everything but the graphics. 
With the same amazing combat mechanics, unique pawn system, & terrible fast travel/revive limitations.

If you have a capable PC pick it up & enjoy, if you don’t lower your expectations & your pc settings. Or go play it on consoles. It is a very demanding game.

I recommend the following mods:
Dullahan
Durnehviir
Weapon lord
Unofficial patch.

It's not what you think it is nor what it's early hours convinces you that it is. You think it's a story-based RPG where all these opportunities open itself up to you, because that is what it tries to make you believe. But.. it creates a promise it cannot keep.

Fundamentally, it is fun. And for several hours it is fun. So I recommend it. But if you expect what it tries to make you expect then you will not enjoy it. Because it gives up in the acts it promises, and never actually becomes the game you want it to be.
great combat and graphics but horrible story line. this should be a 20$ game at most.

When this first released, the port to port was not good, and NPC sprites behavior and data was heavily processed with the CPU, but a lot of patches during 2025 End of Year were done to address this. I have this on Series X, PS5, and now PC because its functionable enough to be played and enjoyed now. I love it!
I just finished the game. Its a nice game with intresting mechanics. Its ok to buy at sale

Overall, I enjoyed Dragon's Dogma 2 as much as I enjoyed the first one and it's DLC, Dark Arisen. There is an improvement by taking the game to a much bigger open world than it's predecessor, however, the monster roster feels a bit small in comparison to DDDA, making it a bit repetitive with lack of variety. 
Regarding performance, I will say that you need to find the sweet sport for your rig an not ultra max settings, unlimited framerate everything. For the record, I played with high settings, 60 fps capped, DLSS balanced and only on 3 especific locations I encountered framerate drops and/or stuttering, the rest of the game ran just fine. I clearly know that at first when it launched was a much worse disaster in terms of optimization since the RE Engine is not suited for open world type of games.

I recommend this game and if you want to wait it out for a sale then that's up to you. 
Cheers!

[h2]Liked:[/h2]
Character creation is great lots to choose from
Map is massive and doesn't get too boring traversing it
Graphics are amazing but you'll need a decent PC 
Combat is decent when you unlock more abilities for your role

[h2]Disliked:[/h2]
Story is genuinely terrible
Voice acting is pretty bad as well
Not many monster designs you'll see the same stuff over and over
There is a lot of time wasting in the game that means you'll be running around a lot without a way to transport due to limited Ferrystones
Can be extremely buggy at times and is frustrating

All in all, I give it a 7/10. It was worth playing and I got my monies worth. I got all the achievements but there were times when I was annoyed with the game. I would recommend it if you have the patience for a slow open world game.

As expected from a Capcom game, the combat system in Dragon’s Dogma 2 is absolutely top tier. Every vocation feels satisfying to play, and combat has that same chaotic, weighty feeling that made Dark Arisen so memorable.

Questing is also a lot more approachable this time around. Compared to Dark Arisen, the game gives better guidance without completely removing the sense of exploration, which makes the overall experience far less frustrating.

PC optimization at launch was rough, especially in cities, but recent patches seem to have fixed most of the major issues for me.

Now I’m just waiting for the DLC announcement. (Hopefully :P

The Game at its core is amazing but it has a long way to go. the story lines has a lot of potential that could have been explored more.  Feels incomplete and at the moment the best way to play it is using mods that balances and introduces new content for the game.
Really loving this game so far. Yeah there are some NPC pop ins when you are in the big cities but overall it runs well.

I really enjoyed this game. The story, dialogue, voice acting -- all ok. Edible.

The combat is just incredible fun. Repetitive, but fun. It has a lot of physicality and weight to it. A constant tavern brawl with limitless chaos. With endgame gear you will experience it far less, which is a shame.

Bad FOV customization. I can only play for a short time before getting sick.

Yes i know there are mods that can improve the FOV, but still, for the price of almost 1 million rupiah (in my country currency, which is very very pricey btw) or around 50 usd AND from a AAA dev, yet it has bad FOV customization is just ridiculous.

The sequel(?) to a game i absolutely loved.
The combat is still great, one of the selling points of the game, but less of it for some reason.

The story feels severely incomplete, or perhaps very, very poorly executed. 
The first half of the game actually had me hooked, but after a while it suddenly just drops the plot and sends you to the "second half" of the game, which ends pretty abruptly.

Monster variety is lacking. You spend the majority of the game fighting goblins, saurians and harpies. And the distance between encounters are so short that you often end up fighting the next group of monsters while fighting the first group. It gets tedious pretty quickly. 

Bosses in this game are, as expected, great. There's less of them, with less variety than the first game, which is a shame. But they're fun to fight. The biggest issue with bosses is actually Grigori, the dragon. In the first game, you spent the majority of the game preparing for the fight, which was very well-designed. It was intense, climatic, more structured and, in my opinion, felt more emotional.
Dragon's Dogma 2's Grigori fight felt rushed, anticlimatic and honestly pretty abrupt. 
Spoiler-ish: The actual battle being optional was such a bad decision aswell. End of spoiler!
Also, where is our Ur-Dragon fight? Give it back!

The new classes are interesting. Spearhand clearly being the most fun (to me), with Wayfarer feeling like this game's version of DD1's Assassin, but able to mix and match between all classes.
Trickster was not my cup of tea, and from what friends told me, it's a very annoying class to play.
The decision to split DD1's Strider(shortbow, daggers) and Ranger (Longbow, daggers) into DD2's Thief and Archer was a bad one, in my own opinion. Each class still felt unique in the first game, and were, again in my opinion, more fun to play.
Removal of Mystic Knight was also a poor decision.

This game could've been so much better, and deserved much better. The community deserved better, after years of hoping for a sequel. If an expansion ever comes out that somehow fixes the base game, or provides us with a much more worthwhile experience, i might change this review into a positive one. 
But for now, Dragon's Dogma 2 might look better and in some areas, play better, but i consider it a worse game than Dragon's Dogma Dark Arisen.

Wow, I should've listened to the reviews. The game feels like a massive downgrade from the first, I just loved the first one so much (Bitterblack speedruns) that i thouhght i would give it a try anyways. Dont do it.
The fact that you cant load saves means you can accidentally really ♥♥♥♥ yourself over and theres nothing you can do about it

This game lowkey kinda slaps. Preformance without upscaling is a bit of an issue, i got arround 30-40 fps out in the open world (PC specs attached), 50-60 with quality upscaling. But holy moly the fights against large enemies are awesome, the only other game that has ever had a similar feel for me are the Monster Hunter series games. Also the title drop is ABSOLUTE CINEMA.

Do not play this like a typical RPG. This game is for exploration and critical thinking. The developer created it with the intent that following the main storyline without exploring or talking to random NPCs inevitably leads to a dissatisfying ending. Take your time playing this; explore everything, enjoy it while it lasts, and don't be fooled by the fake ending. Play by your own free will: however YOU want; not how the main NPCs tell you to.  Defy what is typical and do your best to make your own ending, and you will understand the true masterpiece that this game is and feel completely satisfied when you reach the real ending; understanding what it means.

The sequel to greatest 7/10 game still feels like a 7/10 game. While the sequel improves upon few things there are not nearly enough improvements. Only reason to play 1st game was for combat and that still true for this game. So if you like combat of 1st game then would recommend trying this one sale if your computer is good enough to run it.
suddenly crashing out of nowhere. should be fine with my current setup but no, the game is so bad at optimizing....

Game will run when you first install it then will black screen every time you try after you reset or turn off your machine meaning the you'll need to deleted your config.ini and shader cache every time you wish to play (if you're lucky and that's the fix that will get it to work that time). I wasted tooo much time doing this...

 Shame because when it did work it was very enjoyable but after wasting my whole weekend trying different fixes I gave up uninstalled annoying well after I could refund it.
Runs a lot better than expected, even though in cities there's frame drops compared to the wilderness, its nowhere near unplayable and the combat is peak
Game is utterly broken now; freezes within moments of loading the game world even with 32GB RAM and an RTX 5090 running the game off SSD. Waste of your money.
this game has the charm of as if it was from 2010 somehow, bit clunky everywhere i dont know how they managed this. love it

[b]The game feels rushed and unfinished, and it really shows.[/b]

In my opinion, the first game was a masterpiece that was only limited by the technology of its time. This game, however, feels more like an imitation of it rather than a true successor.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]The Story and Pacing[/h1]

First of all — it’s... short. The story can be completed in around 15 hours in first playthrough, and the worst part is that you can almost do it accidentally.

I finished the game in about 43 hours total (though some of that time was AFK). During the last 3 hours — which included the entire post-game — I kept thinking:

[i]“Is this really it? That’s the ending already?”[/i]

For the first 20 hours, the game felt huge and exciting. It seemed like there was so much to explore and discover. But the final hours feel extremely rushed, especially the story progression.

And speaking of the story — it’s genuinely confusing.

At one point, the game completely shifts your objectives, and suddenly we start helping the bad guy without any proper explanation. Maybe I missed something, but to me it felt like this:

[list]
[*]“This guy is evil, we need to stop him.”
[*]“We prepare to ruin his plans.”
[*]“Some unrelated major story event happens.”
[*]“Now we’re saving the bad guy and helping him continue his evil plan.”
[/list]

Like… what?

Why are we helping him now? What changed?

The transition makes absolutely no sense.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Graphics and Performance[/h1]

The graphics are good overall.

Performance isn’t perfect, but I managed to get a mostly stable 60 FPS on high settings without frame generation, with occasional drops to 30–40 FPS in some areas. It was still manageable.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Vocations and Combat[/h1]

The vocations are disappointing.

Both Thief and Mystic Spearhand basically have an “I’m immortal” button with almost no drawbacks, and Thief deals absurd amounts of damage.

My biggest issue, though, is the limit of only 4 active skills.

Take Thief as an example:

[list]
[*]Every player will take the immortality skill because it’s simply too strong.
[*]Then you need a main damage skill, and most players will choose Helm Splitter because there aren’t many better options.
[*]That already leaves only 2 skill slots.
[/list]

And what do you use those last slots for?

Probably more damage skills, because utility skills feel pointless when you already have an immortality button.

Why use invisibility?
Why place bombs that require setup?
Why experiment with utility at all?

You can just spam stronger damage abilities instead.

And one of your remaining slots practically has to go to the hook ability because fighting flying enemies without it is annoying.

So in the end, build variety feels extremely limited despite the game pretending to offer freedom.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Final Thoughts[/h1]

The first game had many flaws, but I always saw them as part of its atmosphere, technical limitations, or necessary compromises.

And I could forgive those flaws because Dragon’s Dogma 1 delivered a truly unique experience. There’s honestly no other game quite like it.

Sadly, Dragon’s Dogma 2 doesn’t come close to capturing that same feeling.
Play the first one. If your IQ is above room temp, don't waste your time.
EldenRing for those who find EldenRing too difficult.
Camping makes you fail quests that you just spent over an hour on.. 'nuff said
It's a worse version of Dark Arisen in every conceivable way.

yes, definetely

with DLSS i get around 100 fps, in a city around 70 fps
Really immersive and there's lots of great mods available!
